description_long: This is a double-ended open wrench, constructed from a solid piece of metal, likely steel, with a dark, slightly glossy finish. Each end features a U-shaped open jaw, designed for gripping and turning nuts or bolts of specific sizes. The shaft is straight and slender, with a slight outward flare at both ends where the jaws are located. The surface appears smooth with some subtle textural marks, possibly from manufacturing or light use, but no significant grip pattern is visible. The overall form is utilitarian and robust, suitable for mechanical or repair tasks in urban environments such as garages or workshops. There are no visible brand markings, rubberized sections, or decorative elements.
description: A double-ended open wrench made of dark metal with smooth, slightly glossy surfaces and U-shaped jaws at both ends. The shaft is straight and unadorned, with no visible grip pattern or branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wrench, both open jaws clearly visible at each end.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting one open jaw and the flat, broad shape of the wrench's head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the full length and both jaws from the other side.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite end, showing the other open jaw and the flat head profile.
